title: Bash and a Tough Math Puzzle

content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Bash and a Tough Math Puzzle". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/914/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case contains an integer n on the first line. The next line contains n integers a1, a2, ..., an.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of operations required to make all elements of the array equal.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use the concept of Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) to find the minimum number of operations required.

code.Python3:
import math
for _ in range(int(input())):
    n = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    g = arr[0]
    for i in range(1, n):
        g = math.gcd(g, arr[i])
    ans = 0
    for i in range(n):
        ans += arr[i] // g - 1
    print(ans)
